description: Full-length practice test covering all GPAT subjects with comprehensive questions
instructions: This test contains 5 multiple-choice questions (sample format).
There is negative marking of 1 mark for each incorrect answer.
You can navigate between questions using the question palette.
Questions can be marked for review and revisited later.
The timer will show remaining time. Test will auto-submit when time expires.
Click 'Submit Test' when you are ready to finish.

questions.subject: Pharmacology
questions.question_text: Which of the following is a selective beta-1 adrenergic blocker?
questions.explanation: Atenolol is a cardioselective (beta-1) adrenergic blocker. Propranolol is non-selective (blocks both beta-1 and beta-2). Labetalol and carvedilol are combined alpha and beta blockers.

questions.subject: Pharmaceutical Chemistry
questions.explanation: The IUPAC systematic name of aspirin is 2-acetoxybenzoic acid. Acetylsalicylic acid is the chemical/common name, which is also widely used.

questions.subject: Pharmaceutics
questions.question_text: Which of the following is an advantage of sustained-release formulations?
questions.explanation: Sustained-release formulations offer multiple advantages: reduced dosing frequency (once or twice daily), improved patient compliance due to convenience, and decreased side effects due to avoiding peak plasma concentrations.

questions.subject: Pharmaceutical Analysis
questions.question_text: In UV-Visible spectroscopy, bathochromic shift refers to:
questions.explanation: Bathochromic shift (red shift) refers to a shift in absorption maximum to a longer wavelength (lower energy). Hypsochromic shift is the opposite. Hyperchromic and hypochromic refer to intensity changes.

questions.subject: Pharmacognosy
questions.question_text: Quinine, an antimalarial drug, is obtained from:
questions.explanation: Quinine is obtained from the bark of Cinchona tree species (Cinchona officinalis). Digitalis yields cardiac glycosides, opium poppy yields morphine, and Rauwolfia yields reserpine.
